title: Numbers Combination
description:
You are given two integers, $N$ and $K$. Determine the number of sequences of integers $(A_1, A_2, \dots , A_N )$ that satisfy

  • $1 ≤ A_i ≤ i$ for $1 ≤ i ≤ N$, and
  • $A_1 + A_2 + \cdots + A_N = K$.

Since the answer can be very large, calculate the answer modulo $998\, 244\, 353$.
input: Input consists of two integers $N$ $K$ ($1 ≤ N ≤ 100\, 000$; $N ≤ K ≤ 200\, 000$).
output: Output in a line an integer representing the answer modulo $998\, 244\, 353$.
source: ICPC, Regionals, Asia Pacific, Indonesia, Indonesia National Contest, INC 2023
